note: Rebased from CI run 25152767223 — the same codebase that produced 123 184 bundle-less pages on the previous run (25151657070) produced only 6 850 here, a delta of -116 334. The fluctuation between runs is the smoking gun for the dist/index.html read race: jobsSeoPagesPlugin / ogPagesPlugin / staticPagesPlugin each read the bundle hash independently in their closeBundle, and depending on plugin scheduling vs Vite's writeBundle of the SPA shell, hasSpaBundle is sometimes false → tens of thousands of emits drop the bundle. The 6 850 floor is the 'good case': mostly intentional bridges/redirects + a small residual systematic gap to investigate. Locking the floor here means any future re-occurrence of the race will fail the gate (current > baseline) and block the deploy at the source. Drive this number toward zero by (a) making hasSpaBundle deterministic, (b) refactoring intentional-no-bundle templates to live outside the SCAN scope via SKIP_PATHS.
